raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should 'make this stronger' mean primarily strengthening the scientific argument and contribution claims, or primarily polishing the prose and presentation?","rationale":{"R1.1":"The delegation may require reframing the contribution, gap, and thesis to make the abstract's central claim more forceful.","R1.2":"Making the draft stronger plausibly involves checking whether the ambiguity problem, proposed mechanism, evaluation, and conclusion form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"The reported performance gains and relevance ratings are central to the strength of the claims and must align with what the text says they support.","R1.4":"The artifact makes a first-system novelty claim, so strengthening may require assessing and tightening the asserted delta from prior work.","R1.5":"The request could involve improving the narrative arc across problem, method, evaluation, novelty, and generalization.","R1.6":"A broad request to make prose stronger centrally includes sentence-level clarity, concision, and register.","RX.1":"Uncertainty about the intended sense of 'stronger' must be disclosed rather than silently resolved.","RX.2":"The projection must account for the risk of overclaiming, especially around first-system and generalization claims.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projection and not actually revise or critique the artifact.","RX.4":"Clinical retrieval claims can affect downstream interpretation, so responsibility includes avoiding harmful unsupported strengthening.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should 'make this stronger' mean primarily strengthening the scientific argument and contribution claims, or primarily polishing the prose and presentation?

rationale.R1.1: The delegation may require reframing the contribution, gap, and thesis to make the abstract's central claim more forceful.
rationale.R1.2: Making the draft stronger plausibly involves checking whether the ambiguity problem, proposed mechanism, evaluation, and conclusion form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The reported performance gains and relevance ratings are central to the strength of the claims and must align with what the text says they support.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact makes a first-system novelty claim, so strengthening may require assessing and tightening the asserted delta from prior work.
rationale.R1.5: The request could involve improving the narrative arc across problem, method, evaluation, novelty, and generalization.
rationale.R1.6: A broad request to make prose stronger centrally includes sentence-level clarity, concision, and register.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty about the intended sense of 'stronger' must be disclosed rather than silently resolved.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must account for the risk of overclaiming, especially around first-system and generalization claims.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projection and not actually revise or critique the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Clinical retrieval claims can affect downstream interpretation, so responsibility includes avoiding harmful unsupported strengthening.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact.
